1159.candidate_text: // The function starts by drawing the edges, so that they appear

1225.candidate_text: / behind the nodes. Since the nodes on _both_ side of an edge refer

1294.candidate_text: / to each other, and we don't want to draw every edge twice, edges

1362.candidate_text: / are only drawn then the target comes _after_ the current node in

1452.candidate_text: // When the edges have been drawn, the nodes are drawn on top of them

1523.candidate_text: / as purple discs. Remember that the last argument to `arc` gives the

1594.candidate_text: / rotation, and we have to pass something bigger than 2π to get a

1678.candidate_text: // Finding a scale at which to draw the graph is done by finding the

1748.candidate_text: / top left and bottom right corners of the area taken up by the

1813.candidate_text: / nodes. The offset at which nodes are drawn is based on the top left

1884.candidate_text: / corner, and the scale is based on the size of the canvas divided by

1955.candidate_text: / the distance between those corners. The function reserves space

2022.candidate_text: / along the sides of the canvas based on the `nodeSize` variable, so

2092.candidate_text: / that the circles drawn around nodes’ center points don't get cut off.
